attacking_style: Initiates moves early on climbs, often animating breakaways or attacking on penultimate difficulties rather than waiting for final decisive moments. Lacks explosive power for steep finishing gradients.
team_tactics: Used as pace-setter for protected climbers (e.g., Hershey at Pologne) rather than sole leader. Given freedom to animate races early when team has multiple cards to play.
terrain_preference: Long, sustained climbs where recent stage-winning form can be leveraged. Struggles on explosive steep finishes (Ans gradient, Strade Bianche finale) and extreme heat conditions.
vulnerability: Cannot sustain efforts when races fragment on final climbs. Explosive finishing gradients expose insufficient punch. Extreme heat causes complete collapse after early animation.
key_patterns: Attacks early on climbs but fades when decisive moves occur (3 instances: Lombardia, Strade Bianche, Vuelta)
Insufficient finishing power on steep gradients despite strong climbing base (2 instances: LBL 2017, 2019)
Recent stage wins translate to aggressive race animation but not victory (2 instances: Lombardia, LBL 2019)
Crashes and extreme conditions derail promising GC positions (2 instances: Vuelta crash, Strade heat)
form_trajectory: neutral: deployed in domestique role for UAE younger talent suggests career shift from personal ambition to team servitude despite previous climbing wins
